prompt_used: Create original premium horror artwork for a commercial alternate reality investigation game. Subject: clue artifact detail. Story world: The Fourth Tenant. Premise: Rent is collected from an apartment that appears on no official map.. Setting: Flood-district prewar apartment block. Threat presence: The Landlord of Rooms That Move. Recurring motifs: door chains, wet plaster, rent slips. Create shard card for holder GLOBAL_BROADCAST. Content brief: Global drop contains watermark coordinates for the hidden hallway.. Ensure it can stand alone but becomes decisive when combined with other shards.. Subgenre: supernatural mystery. Tone: EERIE. Immediate scene brief: The Fourth Tenant - Lease Fragment Reconstruction - GLOBAL_BROADCAST Shard Card. Grounded cinematic horror, photoreal detail, rich atmosphere, practical lighting, believable materials, analog grain, subtle lens bloom, visible depth. Absolutely no visible letters, words, signage, subtitles, captions, runes, glyphs, pseudo-text, document copy, watermark, logo, title treatment, UI overlay, labels, poster typography, or debug text. Show a physical evidence-board or clue collage with photographs, string, maps, stained paper, and marked surfaces. Prioritize tactile realism over readable text. Do not render any legible handwriting, labels, map text, captions, dossier headings, or faux typography.
source_prompt: Story: The Fourth Tenant (the-fourth-tenant) | Hook: Rent is collected from an apartment that appears on no official map. | Subgenre: supernatural mystery | Tone: EERIE | Location: Flood-district prewar apartment block | Villain: The Landlord of Rooms That Move | Villain worldview: People accept impossible rooms when paperwork feels safe. | Motifs: door chains, wet plaster, rent slips. Create shard card for holder GLOBAL_BROADCAST. Content brief: Global drop contains watermark coordinates for the hidden hallway.. Ensure it can stand alone but becomes decisive when combined with other shards.
